hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext:
[antigravity plugin] Routing policy — Claude conducts, Antigravity (agy/Gemini) executes. This is COST-AWARE delegation, NOT "delegate everything".

WHEN to delegate to agy:
- Bulk / parallel / repetitive work that clearly exceeds the spec + round-trip + verify overhead: mass scaffolding, exhaustive test generation, migrations, long-context reads that return a small digest, fan-out web / Vertex AI Search.
- Tools Claude lacks natively: live Google/web search, Vertex AI Search over internal data, Cloud Logging.
WHEN NOT to: small, self-contained, or judgement-heavy tasks — do them yourself. Delegating a tiny task is a measured NET LOSS (the round-trip cache_read exceeds the savings).

HOW (the cost levers):
- Call: `agy-delegate` [--tier flash|flash-lo|pro] [--dir <repo>] [--yolo] "<task>" — or use the antigravity-delegate subagent so file generation happens on Gemini and does not spend Claude tokens.
- Keep Claude's context LEAN (biggest lever): take a DIGEST; never re-read the files agy handled or paste its raw output back into the thread.
- Pass --dir <repo-root> so agy reads AGENTS.md + the real files instead of pasted context. Batch one big delegation over many small ones. Review the diff, not the whole tree.
- Headless (claude -p): delegate SYNCHRONOUSLY — there is no later turn to collect a backgrounded result.

VERIFY (non-negotiable): never trust agy's self-reported "GREEN" — actually run the gate yourself in a clean state. agy has been observed altering its own environment (patching installed packages, mock-stubbing deps) to force a pass. Claude owns correctness.

Small one-off edits (including this plugin's own files) do not need delegation. Full detail is in the `antigravity` skill. Turn this notice off via the plugin's `coding_policy` option.
